summary:
Great! The fix worked perfectly. The test now shows all the international characters correctly displayed as "Renée Duböis", "Hans Müller", "María García", etc., instead of the corrupted "RenÃ©e DubÃ¶is" format. All 12 name checks pass, confirming that the needle (the encoding issue) has been found and fixed.

The problem was in `/app/app/CsvReader.java` where the constructor was using `Charset.forName("ISO-8859-1")` instead of `Charset.forName("UTF-8")`. This caused international characters to be misinterpreted when reading the CSV file, leading to the corruption shown in the test output.

By changing the charset to UTF-8, the application now properly handles the international characters in the CSV file and displays them correctly in the output report.
